parsed_review.summary: CP00-781 (Migration Platform RP25 permission-and-audit failure-recovery binding, Risk A, 10 units, RP25.P07.M05.S01-RP25.P07.M05.S10) is a descriptor-only continuation pack whose pack-level production_ready claim (migration_platform_failure_recovery_audit_binding_descriptor_verified) is supported by independent read-only inspection. Verified via Read/Grep/Glob only (no command re-execution; exit codes and test counts are read from the recorded command-evidence.json): (1) identity agrees across MIGRATION_PLATFORM_CP781_PACK_BINDING (registry.js:434), manifest plan_binding_snapshot, review-baseline, the regenerated contracts/migration-platform-contract.json latest_pack/latest_projection, and the live next-pack-queue.json (live_latest_pack_id CP00-781, next CP00-782/RP25.P07.M05.S11). (2) CP781_REQUIREMENTS deliverable_counts (failure_recovery:9, security_audit:1), micro_phase_row_counts (RP25.P07.M05:10), and micro_title_row_counts (Permission And Audit Binding:10) match the manifest units and recorded coverage summary exactly; the 10 unit titles map 1:1 to PERMISSION_MATRIX_FAILURE_ROWS.slice(0,10) with S08 correctly typed security_audit. (3) descriptor/case-set/Hermes/Claude/handoff builders (service.js:1706) and validators (validators.js:2239) follow the established CP756-780 pattern with correct substitutions, are wildcard-exported through index.js, wired into validate-rp25-migration-platform-contract.mjs:274, and covered by tests asserting coverage errors [], descriptor errors [], section_count 1, runtime-closed flags, authority boundary, and latest contract projection. (4) recorded command-evidence shows npm test 3027/3027 pass 0 fail and all required validation commands (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) at exit_code 0 with no non-zero exits. (5) regenerated contract records validation.packs.cp781 coverage.valid=true and descriptor.valid=true with empty errors. (6) no-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced via MIGRATION_PLATFORM_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION, descriptor_only/runtime_execution:false per-row flags, and validator guards rejecting source-system/import runtime, permission-decision/cross-tenant exposure, audit event bodies, UI runtime rendering, unauthorized count leaks, and unverified knowledge creation. (7) authority boundary keeps Claude read-only and non-final (claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false), requires human approval for runtime opening, sets local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false and runtime_opening_pack_id CP00-782, with runtime_ready:false (no runtime/enterprise-trust over-claim). (8) Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor-only review packet/receipt handling (emits_runtime_receipt:false, receipt_shape descriptor_only_migration_no_write), not product runtime. (9) CP178+ review hardening holds: auth preflight passed (readiness-report), invalid_review_attempts empty, and the forbidden_review_evidence guard list (not_logged_in, malformed_json, usage_limit, tool_call_shaped_output, zero_byte_stdout, fenced_json_with_prose) is defined and referenced by CP781. The PENDING/production_ready:false states in manifest.json, construction-inspection.json, adjudication.md, and claude-review-result.json are the expected pre-review seeded state this receipt gates, not defects. Changed-file scope was respected: unrelated dirty/untracked files (LDIP, workbook, progress-control-room, docx) were not attributed to this pack; the closeout-pack-plan.json/next-pack-queue.json modifications are expected post-snapshot plan regeneration. One non-blocking P3: the manifest worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved contains a malformed, inert path string ontracts/migration-platform-contract.json (systemic, also present in CP780) that matches no real file; the authoritative changed-file-scope.json correctly classifies the contract as a pack implementation ref, so closeout is unaffected. blocks_goal_closeout=false because the pack does not claim runtime or enterprise trust within this receipt; the human/enterprise-trust final-approval boundary is expected and outside this CP closeout receipt. Claude acted strictly as a read-only independent reviewer and is not the final approval authority.

findings.title: Manifest unrelated_dirty_files_preserved contains malformed/inert contract path
findings.details: worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved lists 'ontracts/migration-platform-contract.json' (missing leading 'c'). This is a truncated/typo'd form of contracts/migration-platform-contract.json, which is in fact a pack implementation file (correctly listed in active_pack_required_untracked_files at manifest.json:205 and in the authoritative artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-781/changed-file-scope.json active_pack_implementation_refs). The defect is inert: the typo'd path matches no real file, so the real contract is not misclassified as unrelated and is staged with the pack. The same artifact appears in the CP780 manifest, indicating a systemic, pre-existing generator quirk rather than something introduced by CP00-781. No functional, test, boundary, or validation gate is affected.
findings.recommendation: In the manifest generator, remove the contract entry from unrelated_dirty_files_preserved entirely (the contract is a pack file, not unrelated) and fix the truncated path; reconcile this list with the authoritative changed-file-scope.json classification. Non-blocking for CP00-781 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking observation recorded for maintainer/control-plane follow-up. Does not block pack or goal closeout; the authoritative changed-file-scope.json classification is correct and all closeout gates pass.
